reconstruction_test: If 2.35 were removed, could the engineering analog be reconstructed from 2.34 (imperishable disrepute) plus 2.36 (vācyāḥ avācya-vādān — unspeakable insults)?
result: No — 2.34 names imperishable disrepute among bhūtāni (all beings); 2.36 names the speech itself (the unspeakable things they will say). Neither names the competent-peer-class restriction (mahā-rathāḥ) that 2.35 distinctly carries. The bahu-mataḥ-to-lāghavam phase-change framing — that the actor moves from 'highly esteemed in the network' to 'lightness' — is irreducibly 2.35's. The Kubernetes-operator + sabbatical scenario specifically requires the competent-peer frame; without 2.35's mahā-rathāḥ, the scenario would slide into the broader bhūtāni frame of 2.34.
verdict_reason: The engineering layer carries 2.35-specific doctrinal load (mahā-rathāḥ peer-class, bahu-mataḥ-to-lāghavam phase change, bhayāt-default in peer cognition) that cannot be reconstructed from neighbours. PASS.
